key_insight: KOAL improves prostate cancer Gleason Grade Group prediction by combining MRI with clinical variables, expert knowledge from reports/guidelines, and ordinal-aware grading logic.
clinical_relevance: Better non-invasive GGG estimation from mpMRI could help reduce unnecessary biopsies and improve risk stratification in prostate cancer.
method: Clinical-Context Modulation uses age/PSA to adapt image features.
Knowledge-Guided Prototype Alignment turns LLM-extracted radiology/guideline knowledge into offline semantic anchors.
Hierarchical Ordinal-aware Constraints predict Gleason primary/secondary patterns and map them to GGG with a differentiable logic layer.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that KOAL outperforms state-of-the-art methods on public PI-CAI and an in-house dataset.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and generalizability of extracted knowledge anchors and clinical data, so external validation and workflow integration are important.
paper_title: KOAL: Knowledge-Driven Prostate Cancer Grading with Ordinal-Aware Learning
